summary: Treatment duration significantly impacts patient-reported quality-of-life measures and treatment logistics; although survival is maintained across durations, shorter courses improve compliance and reduce disease-related burdens through advanced techniques like hypofractionation and SABR. Therefore, yes, treatment duration does influence clinically meaningful outcomes even when oncological effectiveness remains unchanged.
